extraction.one_line_summary: BALMANN Koen won from 7-rider breakaway after spending time as virtual maglia rosa on 4000m+ climbing stage, attacking repeatedly final 30km.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo successfully defended pink with Formolo in break, avoiding full team chase while controlling gap under virtual jersey threshold.
Ineos prioritized preventing virtual maglia rosa change over break composition, revealing jersey defense takes precedence over stage win opportunities.
Dumoulin repeatedly dropped then bridged in final 30km attacks, suggesting diesel engine recovering form rather than explosive climbing deficit.
Seven-rider break required over 30km formation past first climb, indicating peloton reluctance to release quality riders on 4000m stages.

rider_signals.BALMANN Koen: positive: First GT stage win ever, became virtual rosa mid-stage, dropped at 25km but bridged back, won uphill sprint at 13% gradient
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: neutral: Dropped multiple times in final 30km but consistently bridged back, finished 4th suggesting improving condition but no explosive power
rider_signals.LOPEZ Juanpe: positive: Trek's Formolo breakaway protection allowed Lopez group to ride defensively, successfully neutralizing Kemna's late attack despite limited team strength
rider_signals.VILLELLA Davide: negative: Ineos rider in break dropped earlier than Dumoulin despite team's controlling tempo, suggesting support role exhausted climbing reserves
rider_signals.KEMNA Lennard: negative: Failed to force split despite late attack with Lopez isolated, revealing lack of firepower to exploit numerical disadvantage situations
